block_01: Start your trading week with a sneak peek of upcoming trading opportunities and latest financial markets news with the IQ Trending weekly report.
block_05: After surpassing the $41,000 threshold at the beginning of the year, Bitcoin rolled back finding support levels at $31,971 and $33,168 consistently, and to date, the world-first cryptocurrency is trading around $36,215.
block_06: The second-largest cryptocurrency by market cap, Ether (ETH), surged in price getting close to its all-time high trading around $1,300. As BTC dropped from its new high, Ether price corrected as well, briefly falling below $1,000 before recovering.
block_07: However, not BTC nor Ether became the breakthrough of this past week, but Zcash. ZEC’s price was up by nearly 115% in the past few weeks and has established a new two-year high at $120.224 last Wednesday.
block_09: Last week Pound Sterling hit a seven-week high against the Euro as the Bank of England governor Andrew Bailey rejected negative interest rates, while optimism about the pace of vaccination roll-out in the UK also provided support.
block_10: Euro remained capped as the political climate in Italy got too intense amid criticisms around the proposed governance of the Next Generation EU funds.
block_11: US Dollar gained ground as Treasury yields spike, in addition to the barrage of speeches from Fed officials, and stimulus package proposal from president-elect Joe Biden.
block_13: With the Democrats taking control of the Senate, hopes have increased for looser restrictions on cannabis in the US. Last week marijuana stocks surged in price amid the inauguration of Joe Biden. The new government has already voted to pass the bill that would legalize marijuana federally. In this regard, the industry is expected to grow dramatically in the near future showing profitable results to its investors.
block_17: Japan recorded a trade surplus of JPY 872.9 billion in October of 2020, compared with a JPY 11.2 billion surplus a year earlier and market expectations of a JPY 250 billion surplus. It was the fourth straight month of surplus and the largest since February, as exports fell by 0.2 percent year-on-year to JPY 6.57 billion while imports dropped at a faster 13.3 percent to JPY 5.69 billion. Considering the first ten months of the year, Japan posted a trade gap of JPY 420.5 billion, narrowing from a JPY 1,420.3 billion shortfall in the same period of 2019.
